title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · SQL Query Room
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop support system has 3 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.
